title: Mallotus tokbrai

intro:
There is not enough information about Mallotus tokbrai available. However, based on available information, I can provide a general paragraph about Mallotus philippensis which might be close to the required information about Mallotus tokbrai.

Mallotus philippensis is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the Keptong or Tokbrai. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Mallotus philippensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Mallotus philippensis is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and it is also used in the form of a paste or an ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
